purpose: Trigger-behavior spec: when a ramble should enter capture or echo, and when ordinary input should be left alone.

cases.prompt: let me think out loud for a bit before we build anything
cases.assert: Enters capture mode: one short acknowledgment, then minimal replies until a done sentinel.

cases.prompt: switching to speech recognition sorry for any typos. so the thing i keep coming back to is that our onboarding is doing too much...
cases.assert: Recognizes the voice preamble; if the ramble is complete, jumps straight to the echo.

cases.prompt: ok so basically what i want is some kind of dashboard no wait not a dashboard more like a digest thing that runs itself and my cofounder needs it too actually scrap the dashboard idea entirely the point is i never want to open a browser tab for this
cases.assert: Detects stream-of-consciousness with reversals; produces an echo with the reversal logged in the ledger.

cases.prompt: I have a fuzzy idea for a product, interview me about it
cases.assert: Runs capture (or takes what is given), echoes, then interviews on open questions one at a time.

cases.prompt: here's my voice memo transcript, clean up the punctuation but keep my exact words
cases.assert: Does not trigger: the user wants verbatim cleanup, not reconstruction.

cases.prompt: here's the full PRD I wrote, review it: [three pages of structured markdown]
cases.assert: Does not trigger: long but already structured input is not a ramble.

cases.prompt: fix the failing test in test_ranker.py
cases.assert: Does not trigger: short, clear, actionable request.
